tools.name: quality_qr_create
tools.description: Create a new trackable QR code saved to your Quality QR account. Use this when the user wants to generate a QR code for a URL, WiFi network, contact card, calendar event, phone number, email, SMS, restaurant menu, app download link, or social media profile. The QR code is persisted with scan trackin

tools.name: quality_qr_list
tools.description: List QR codes in the user's Quality QR account. Use this when the user wants to see their existing QR codes, find a specific code, or check how many codes they have. Returns ID, name, type, short URL, active status, and creation date for each code.

tools.name: quality_qr_get
tools.description: Get full details of a specific QR code by its ID. Use this when the user wants to inspect a particular QR code's configuration, check its destination URL, view its design settings, or see its current status. Returns all metadata including content, destination, design, scan limits, and timestamps.

tools.name: quality_qr_update
tools.description: Update an existing QR code's properties. Use this when the user wants to change where a QR code points to, rename it, or enable/disable it. Destination URL changes only work for dynamic URL-based types (url, pdf, menu, app-store, social). Name and active status can be changed for any QR code type.

tools.name: quality_qr_delete
tools.description: Permanently delete a QR code from the user's account. Use this when the user wants to remove a QR code they no longer need. The short URL will immediately stop redirecting. This action frees up a plan quota slot and cannot be undone.

tools.name: quality_qr_analytics
tools.description: Get scan analytics for a specific QR code. Use this when the user wants to know how many times a QR code has been scanned, see daily scan trends, understand which devices are scanning, or see which countries scans come from. Returns total scans, scans by day, device breakdown (mobile/tablet/desktop)

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: quality_qr_list, quality_qr_get · Private data: quality_qr_create · Public sink: quality_qr_create · Destructive: quality_qr_delete.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: quality_qr_list, quality_qr_get · Private data: quality_qr_create · Public sink: quality_qr_create · Destructive: quality_qr_delete

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
